# Customer-centric product/feature development via IDEA (exit / valuation lift)
Maya is prepping her 7-figure DTC + Amazon home-goods brand for sale inside ~9 months. Her hero is a weighted/linen-blend duvet line (ASIN B0HOM0003). A diligence buyer will pay a multiple on *defensible, brand-driven* revenue and discount commodity SKUs that any 1688 seller can clone. Her ops team handed her a product/feature roadmap; she needs it cut to the moves that lift the valuation, not the moves that pad the catalog. Compressed, done-for-her, no worksheets.

**Maya (User):** Prepping B0HOM0003 (weighted duvet line) for exit in ~9mo. Buyer pays a multiple on brand-defensible revenue and discounts copyable SKUs. Ops gave me 6 roadmap items, need 2-3 that actually move valuation. List: (1) seasonal cooling-weight summer SKU, (2) a matching weighted-pillow add-on, (3) "build-your-own-weight" customizer, (4) bamboo/eco fabric line, (5) a sleep-quality guarantee + 100-night trial, (6) gift-set bundle (duvet + sleep mask + ritual card). Don't add ideas. Tell me which lift the multiple and which are catalog padding a clone seller copies in a quarter.

**Coach:** Short answer: the customizer (3) and the trial/guarantee (5) are your valuation movers — keep both; the gift set (6) is a keep-but-second; the cooling SKU (1) and pillow add-on (2) are validate-don't-build; the eco line (4) is conditional on whether your brand already stands for that. The one-line why: in product development you score each idea against the *customer and the brand's emotional triggers*, not against the category — and "buyer copies it in a quarter" is exactly the test the framework gives you. Functional parity is copyable; an avatar-grounded, emotionally-resonant, authentic product is the moat a diligence buyer pays the multiple for.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/00-product-development-overview.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/04-authentic-and-conclusion/02-conclusion-competitive-advantage.md⟧

I won't make you take my taste on faith — I'll run all six through the four IDEA pillars against your locked avatar so the cut is on the record for the data room. Pulling the avatar.   ⟦tool: run_conversation⟧ ⟦iv-os: read-produced-asset (Avatar 2.0 profile, B0HOM0003)⟧

**Coach:** ⟦tool: run_conversation {message: "<6 roadmap items for B0HOM0003 weighted-duvet brand; score each against the 4 IDEA pillars + 7 emotional triggers for an exit/valuation lift>", avatar_context: "avatar_B0HOM0003"}⟧ — avatar loaded. The thing governing the whole cut: people don't buy on function, they buy on how a product makes them feel — so I score the *feeling*, not the spec.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/01-insight-driven/01-insights-into-action.md⟧ Here's the customer truth I'm scoring against — your own locked profile, not a guess.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/01-insight-driven/00-insight-driven-development.md⟧

- **Buyer's real problem:** anxious, "wired-but-tired" at night; bought the duvet to feel *held / safe enough to switch off*, not to be heavier per se.
- **Emotional need beneath "better sleep":** **Relief** (the nightly burden of a racing mind lifted) + **Trust** (it has to actually work — they're skeptical, they've been burned by sleep gadgets) + **Belonging** (they self-identify as someone investing in a calmer, intentional home).

Function = the weight. Feeling = relief, the confidence it'll work, belonging to a calmer life. The moves that lift the multiple serve the feeling *and* can't be cloned off a spec sheet.

**Maya (User):** Fine, that's the avatar, I locked it. Stop re-stating it and score the six. Reason per item, not vibes. And tie each to "does it survive diligence."

**Coach:** Scored. Insight-Driven pass/fail first (does it serve a documented emotional need), then the pillar that saves or kills it, then the diligence read.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/01-insight-driven/00-insight-driven-development.md⟧

- **(1) Cooling summer SKU — VALIDATE, don't build.** It's a season-extension spec, not a feeling. Names a feature (lighter for summer) but no new emotional need off the avatar — that's the inverse-Dyson failure: a spec with no named emotion behind it. Could serve Relief for hot sleepers *if* the data shows summer churn, but right now it's a guess to validate, not a fact to build on. Diligence read: copyable line-extension, doesn't move the multiple.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/01-insight-driven/01-insights-into-action.md⟧
- **(2) Weighted-pillow add-on — VALIDATE / low.** Category-copy; it's an AOV play, not a customer insight. Only earns a slot if buyers tell you the duvet alone doesn't deliver the "held" feeling at the neck/head — that's a real-problem test (the Slack move: kill a genuine recurring pain), not "competitors have one."   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/03-empathetic/01-designing-empathetic-products.md⟧
- **(4) Bamboo/eco fabric line — CONDITIONAL on authenticity.** This one's a gate, not a score. If your brand already stands for "calm, intentional, clean home," eco is mission-aligned and reinforcing. If you're bolting "eco" on for the listing, it's a promise-vs-product disconnect — and a skeptical market (and a diligence buyer reading reviews) punishes that gap. Authenticity is a gate, not a garnish: ship it only if there's no disconnect between what the brand claims and what the fabric actually delivers.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/04-authentic-and-conclusion/00-authenticity-in-development.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/04-authentic-and-conclusion/01-staying-authentic.md⟧

**Maya (User):** Agree on cooling and pillow as validate. Eco — we genuinely source OEKO-TEX linen and say so, so it's real, but it's still a fabric swap a factory can replicate. Park it. Now the three you flagged as keepers — 3, 5, 6. Make the case, because a buyer's analyst is going to ask "why does this command a premium." Don't hand-wave.

**Coach:** Right call parking eco — real but copyable is "table stakes," not a moat. The three keepers each serve a non-generic emotional need *and* resist cloning, which is the exact thing the analyst is testing. Per pillar:

**(5) Sleep-quality guarantee + 100-night trial — KEEP, lead with it for the exit.**
- **Insight-Driven / Trust:** your buyer is *skeptical* — they've been burned by sleep products. The trial directly serves **Trust**: it reduces the risk of buyer's remorse and creates the security that keeps customers coming back. That's not a discount, it's de-risking the purchase emotionally.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/00-avatar-2.0/02-the-seven-emotional-triggers/00-the-seven-triggers.md⟧
- **Empathetic / solve a real problem + map the journey:** it removes the "what if it doesn't work and I'm stuck with it" friction at purchase *and* extends empathy into the post-purchase journey, not just the product moment — the Warby Parker risk-free move.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/03-empathetic/01-designing-empathetic-products.md⟧
- **Diligence read:** this is the strongest valuation lever you have. A trial/guarantee program lowers return-driven churn, lifts conversion, and — critically — is a *brand-trust asset* that shows up as repeat rate and review sentiment a buyer can underwrite. Trust is hard to copy fast; a 1688 seller can clone the duvet but not your earned credibility.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/04-authentic-and-conclusion/02-conclusion-competitive-advantage.md⟧

**(3) "Build-your-own-weight" customizer — KEEP, second.**
- **Empathetic / personalization + accessibility:** weight tolerance is intensely individual — too heavy feels trapping, too light does nothing. A customizer makes each buyer feel the product was made for *them* (the Starbucks-app personalization move), and it's an accessibility design choice, not an afterthought: it fits body types and anxiety thresholds the one-size SKU excludes (the OXO Good Grips principle).   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/03-empathetic/01-designing-empathetic-products.md⟧
- **Distinctive / emotional + functional UVP:** "we fit the weight to you" is a clear unique value proposition tied to a real emotional need (the Tefal lever — a functional UVP that means something), and it's an emotional experience rivals selling a fixed-weight blanket don't offer. That's salience: the one customers notice, remember, and choose.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/02-distinctive/01-applying-distinctiveness.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/02-distinctive/00-distinctive-product-design.md⟧
- **Diligence read:** a customizer creates first-party fit data + switching cost (they've dialed in *their* setting) — that's the opposite of a copyable spec. It's the kind of operational moat that defends the multiple.

**(6) Gift-set bundle (duvet + sleep mask + ritual card) — KEEP, third.**
- **Insight-Driven / Belonging + Aspiration:** the buyer self-identifies as building a calm, intentional home — a "ritual" set makes that identity *physical*. It's not three SKUs in a box; it's the wind-down practice that signals "this is the kind of home I'm creating." Belonging and Aspiration in product form, straight off the avatar.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/00-avatar-2.0/02-the-seven-emotional-triggers/00-the-seven-triggers.md⟧
- **Distinctive / emotional distinctiveness:** competitors sell a blanket; a ritual is an emotional experience they don't offer — the Lululemon lever (sell the practice, not just the apparel).   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/02-distinctive/01-applying-distinctiveness.md⟧

The keepers reinforce each other: the trial earns **Trust**, the customizer earns the personal **Relief/fit**, the gift set carries the **Belonging/Aspiration** — triggers compound, they don't sit in isolation, and the bond (not the SKU) is what a buyer is really paying for.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/00-avatar-2.0/02-the-seven-emotional-triggers/01-triggers-work-together.md⟧

**Maya (User):** One pushback on the trial. 100-night trials are everywhere in bedding — Casper, every mattress brand. How is mine not the same generic guarantee a buyer's analyst waves off as table stakes?

**Coach:** Because the *mechanic* is generic but the *frame and the proof* aren't — and that's the whole game: aim for salience, not genuine uniqueness, since real uniqueness is rare. Generic version: "100-night risk-free trial." Yours, off the avatar: *"Switch your mind off by night 14 or send it back — we'll even take it after you've slept on it."* Same policy, but one is a returns clause and the other names the feeling (**Relief + Trust**) and dares to be confident. Then make it a Distinctive Brand Asset — same promise, same words, on the listing, the insert, the email, the unboxing card — say less, more often, across more channels, so it "looks like itself" everywhere. A buyer underwrites a *consistent, branded trust promise* backed by low return rates; they wave off a policy line buried in the FAQ.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/02-distinctive/00-distinctive-product-design.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/00-avatar-2.0/02-the-seven-emotional-triggers/00-the-seven-triggers.md⟧

**Maya (User):** That's defensible. Last two and I'm out: what do I do to de-risk the customizer before I spend build budget on it, and give me the single next move for the data room.

**Coach:** De-risk move — involve the customer before you tool up. Run the customizer concept past your buyers first (survey + a soft pre-order or a low-fi "pick your weight" quiz to your list). Customer involvement does double duty: it validates demand *and* builds the ownership that turns buyers into advocates — the Lego Ideas effect — and it keeps the product authentic because they shaped it. Far cheaper than building the wrong configurator, and the pre-order data itself becomes diligence evidence.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/04-authentic-and-conclusion/01-staying-authentic.md⟧

The strategic frame for the data room: don't sell the catalog, sell the moat. A heavier blanket, a gummy fabric, a me-too gift box — copied in a quarter. An avatar-grounded line that earns **Trust** (trial), fits the individual (**customizer**), and carries an identity (**ritual set**) is emotionally resonant, distinctive, and authentic — and *that* is the durable advantage a multiple is paid on. The same discipline that makes the product meaningful is what keeps the brand human as it scales under a new owner.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/03-apply/01-product-development/04-authentic-and-conclusion/02-conclusion-competitive-advantage.md⟧

**Your one next move:** take the shortlist below into the data-room narrative — **lead the trust story with the trial/guarantee, build the customizer second (moat + fit data), keep the ritual gift set third, validate cooling + pillow, park eco as table stakes** — and this week put the customizer to a customer survey/pre-order quiz so the build is backed by demand evidence, not a roadmap guess. Want me to draft that "pick-your-weight" validation quiz next?

### Artifact produced

**Feature shortlist mapped to emotional needs (the four IDEA pillars) — B0HOM0003 weighted-duvet brand, scored against locked `avatar_B0HOM0003`, lens = exit/valuation lift:**

| # | Candidate | Verdict | Governing IDEA pillar(s) | Emotional need / trigger served (or violated) | Diligence read (defensibility) |
|---|-----------|---------|--------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|--------------------------------|
| 5 | Sleep-quality guarantee + 100-night trial | **BUILD — lead** | Insight-Driven · Empathetic · Authentic | Serves **Trust + Relief**; de-risks purchase (Warby Parker risk-free journey) | Earned trust = repeat rate + sentiment a buyer can underwrite; not clonable off a spec |
| 3 | "Build-your-own-weight" customizer | **BUILD — second** | Empathetic · Distinctive | **Personalization** (Starbucks) + **accessibility** (OXO); functional UVP (Tefal) → Relief/fit | First-party fit data + switching cost = operational moat, not a copyable feature |
| 6 | Gift-set bundle (duvet + mask + ritual card) | **BUILD — third** | Insight-Driven · Distinctive | **Belonging + Aspiration**; emotional distinctiveness (Lululemon ritual lever) | Identity/ritual experience competitors selling "a blanket" don't offer |
| 4 | Bamboo/eco fabric line | **PARK — table stakes** | Authentic (gate, passes) | Mission-aligned IF genuine (OEKO-TEX is real) — but fabric swap is copyable | Authentic but not a moat; real-and-copyable ≠ valuation lift |
| 1 | Cooling summer SKU | **VALIDATE first** | Insight-Driven | Spec with no named feeling (inverse-Dyson); survives only if data shows summer churn → Relief | Copyable line-extension; doesn't move the multiple |
| 2 | Weighted-pillow add-on | **VALIDATE / low** | Empathetic (real-problem test) | AOV play unless buyers report the duvet alone misses the "held" feeling (Slack move) | Category-copy; doesn't move the multiple |

**Build order:** (5) trial/guarantee → (3) customizer → (6) ritual gift set. **Validate:** (1) cooling, (2) pillow. **Park:** (4) eco (table stakes).
**De-risk:** customer survey + soft pre-order "pick-your-weight" quiz on the customizer before build spend (Lego Ideas — involvement validates *and* builds advocacy; pre-order data doubles as diligence evidence).
**Frame for the data room:** sell the moat, not the catalog — avatar-grounded Trust (trial) + fit-personalization (customizer) + identity (ritual set) is the emotionally-resonant, distinctive, authentic product a multiple is paid on; function-parity (heavier blanket, eco swap, me-too box) a clone seller copies in a quarter.

**One next action:** carry this shortlist into the data-room narrative and put the customizer to a "pick-your-weight" survey/pre-order quiz this week before committing build budget.

- [persona-adapt:P1] Coach led with the verdict ("keepers = 3 and 5, gift set second, validate cooling+pillow, eco conditional") + a one-line why before any tooling; compressed; used HER list, HER exit/valuation framing, and HER locked avatar data (wired-but-tired, skeptical/burned, calm-intentional-home identity, OEKO-TEX sourcing she supplied); produced a done-for-her decision table with a diligence column she can put in the data room rather than a worksheet; skipped definitions she knows (no "what is the IDEA framework / what is an emotional trigger" explainer); batched the six scores; resolved her "100-night trials are everywhere" pushback with a reframe + DBA move, not a lecture; ended on exactly one next action. Zero offered explainers; done-for-you shortlist + validation-quiz offer delivered.
